title: V3Tristate creates multi-driven enable signal
body:
`t_altera_lpm_counter` after V3Tristate contains a `cin__en` signal that is driven both via an input port and locally in the module:

```
1:2:1: PIN 0x600000139d40 {f3598am}  cin__en -> VAR 0x6000006f3600 {f3598am} @dt=0x600000fb46c0@(G/w1)  cin__en INPUT MODULETEMP
1:2:1:1: VARREF 0x6000003abd40 {f3598am} @dt=0x600000f8d380@(G/w1)  cin__en8 [RV] <- VAR 0x600000696f00 {f3598am} @dt=0x600000f8d380@(G/w1)  cin__en8 MODULETEMP

...

1:2:2:1: VARREF 0x6000003a8e00 {f3598am} @dt=0x600000fb46c0@(G/w1)  cin__out__strong__en [RV] <- VAR 0x6000006f3800 {f3598am} @dt=0x600000fb46c0@(G/w1)  cin__out__strong__en MODULETEMP
1:2:2:2: VARREF 0x6000003a8ee0 {f3598am} @dt=0x600000fb46c0@(G/w1)  cin__en [LV] => VAR 0x6000006f3600 {f3598am} @dt=0x600000fb46c0@(G/w1)  cin__en INPUT MODULETEMP
```

Given the purpose of this pass is to resolve multiple drivers, this appears a bug. Whichever driver wins is indeterminate and depends on downstream optimisations and scheduling.

Edit: I'm not looking to fix this, just recording.
code_blocks:

1:2:1:1: VARREF 0x6000003abd40 {f3598am} @dt=0x600000f8d380@(G/w1)  cin__en8 [RV] <- VAR 0x600000696f00 {f3598am} @dt=0x600000f8d380@(G/w1)  cin__en8 MODULETEMP

...

1:2:2:1: VARREF 0x6000003a8e00 {f3598am} @dt=0x600000fb46c0@(G/w1)  cin__out__strong__en [RV] <- VAR 0x6000006f3800 {f3598am} @dt=0x600000fb46c0@(G/w1)  cin__out__strong__en MODULETEMP
1:2:2:2: VARREF 0x6000003a8ee0 {f3598am} @dt=0x600000fb46c0@(G/w1)  cin__en [LV] => VAR 0x6000006f3600 {f3598am} @dt=0x600000fb46c0@(G/w1)  cin__en INPUT MODULETEMP
